overrides.override_summary: Maximum allowable interest rate capped at 10% per annum (vs. 1.5%/month = 18%/yr in FA)
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly reduces late payment penalty exposure for CA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clause may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code Section 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Termination damages calculation may not be enforceable in CA; franchisor would need to prove actual damages

overrides.override_summary: NC governing law provision might not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to apply CA law which provides stronger franchisee protections

overrides.override_summary: General release may be void under CA Corp Code 31512 (waiver of franchise law rights) and Bus & Prof Code 20010
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may not be required to sign a binding general release upon transfer, preserving their statutory claims

overrides.override_summary: No statement/questionnaire/acknowledgment shall waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects CA franchisees from inadvertently waiving fraud/misrepresentation claims

overrides.override_summary: No exemptions under Hawaii franchise law
overrides.why_it_matters: Full HI franchise law protections apply

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the agreements; IL Franchise Disclosure Act controls
overrides.why_it_matters: IL law replaces NC law, providing IL-specific franchisee protections including registration requirements

overrides.override_summary: Indiana franchise laws govern the FA and all related agreements
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisee protections under IC 23-2-2.7 apply

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration must take place in Indiana
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisees not required to travel to NC for dispute resolution

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring suit in Maryland
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees have home-state litigation option

overrides.override_summary: General release limited to claims with specifically enumerated exceptions under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain statutory franchise law claims despite release

overrides.override_summary: MN law governs; MN Statute 80C controls
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong MN franchisee protections apply including registration requirements

overrides.override_summary: Venue must be in Minnesota
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees not required to travel to NC

overrides.override_summary: 90-day termination notice required; 60-day cure period
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly extends cure period from FA's 30 days to 60 days, and adds 90-day advance termination notice

overrides.override_summary: No general release inconsistent with MN law
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects MN franchisees from waiving statutory rights

overrides.override_summary: NY General Business Law Article 33 controls
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law protections apply

overrides.override_summary: NY venue available for disputes
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees have home-state option

overrides.override_summary: ND law applies
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law protections apply

overrides.override_summary: Covenants not to compete generally unenforceable in ND
overrides.why_it_matters: Moot for this FDD (no noncompete exists), but confirms ND's anti-noncompete stance

overrides.override_summary: ND venue must be available; arbitration site must include ND option
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees not required to travel to NC

overrides.override_summary: Jury trial waiver may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees may retain right to jury trial despite FA waiver

overrides.override_summary: RI Franchise Investment Act protections apply
overrides.why_it_matters: Full RI franchise law protections

overrides.override_summary: VA Retail Franchising Act disclosures
overrides.why_it_matters: VA statutory protections apply

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act governs
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law provides strong franchisee protections

overrides.override_summary: WA venue available for arbitration/litigation
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees not required to travel to NC

overrides.override_summary: No release provision that is unfair
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects WA franchisees from overreaching release requirements

overrides.override_summary: WI Fair Dealership Law (Ch. 135) controls
overrides.why_it_matters: Good cause required for termination; strong franchisee protections under WI law

overrides.override_summary: Good cause required for termination under WI Fair Dealership Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor cannot terminate without good cause in WI, providing significant protection

overrides.override_summary: 90-day notice and 60-day cure period required
overrides.why_it_matters: Extended cure period protects WI franchisees from rapid termination
